hints.Present indicative: Present 3rd sg. of 'stare' would be 'stat' — 'stabat' has the -ba- imperfect marker
hints.Perfect indicative: Perfect 3rd sg. of 'stare' would be 'stetit' — 'stabat' has the -ba- imperfect marker
hints.Pluperfect indicative: Pluperfect 3rd sg. would be 'steterat' — 'stabat' has the -ba- imperfect marker

hints.sum, esse, fui, futurus: These are the principal parts of 'esse' (to be)
hints.sedeo, sedere, sedi, sessum: These are the principal parts of 'sedere' (to sit)
hints.maneo, manere, mansi, mansum: These are the principal parts of 'manere' (to remain)

hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'mater' would be 'matris' — 'mater' is the nominative form
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'mater' would be 'matrem' — 'mater' is the nominative form
hints.Vocative: Vocative of 'mater' is the same as nominative, but here it is the subject of 'stabat'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'matris' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'matris' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'matris' ends in -is

hints.Nominative: Nominative is 'crux' — 'crucem' has the -em accusative ending
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'crux' would be 'crucis' — 'crucem' has the -em accusative ending
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'crux' would be 'cruce' — 'crucem' has the -em accusative ending

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'crucis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'crucis' ends in -is
hints.5th declension: 5th declension nouns have genitive in -ei — 'crucis' ends in -is

translation: The sorrowful mother was standing, tearful, beside the cross
